questions.question: Patient H.M.'s case was groundbreaking because it demonstrated that:

options.text: All types of memory are stored in the hippocampus
Explicit and implicit memory depend on different brain structures
The cerebellum is the primary brain area for all memory formation
Short-term memory and long-term memory use the same neural pathways

questions.explanation: After H.M.'s hippocampal removal, he could no longer form new explicit (declarative) memories but could still learn new procedural skills (implicit memory). This dissociation provided strong evidence that explicit and implicit memory systems are neurologically distinct.

questions.question: Which brain structure is most directly responsible for enhancing the encoding and storage of emotionally charged memories?
questions.explanation: The amygdala plays a critical role in processing emotional memories and enhancing the encoding of emotionally significant events. This is why emotionally charged experiences -- both positive and negative -- tend to be remembered more vividly than neutral events.
